visualGoal: Sell the honest truth of the marine bay: there is no boat wheel, only repurposed and DIY gear gated by analog-axis support. Show verified product proof for everything we CAN prove; flag the two community-default items (racing-wheel helm, flight-throttle quadrant) as text-only rather than faking a photo. No procedural or stock art.

heroImage.note: Bespoke per-article marine bay hero. Exists on disk; intentionally outside verified-product-images.json (that manifest governs /images/gear/ product shots only). Every marine article uses this /images/marine/<slug>.jpg pattern.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The community-default helm stand-in and the highest-value photo to source. A local file exists but is UNVERIFIED, so it was removed from the body; the G29 is now text + link. Verify a real marine-context photo before it can appear as a shotgrid/gearpick visual.
Pricier helm alternative; TrueForce wasted in marine. Local file exists but UNVERIFIED. Text + link only.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Logitech G Saitek Pro Flight Throttle Quadrant (as engine telegraph)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The 'quiet hero' / best-value pick in the bay. Local file exists but UNVERIFIED, so removed from body and shown text + link. Second-highest-priority photo to source after the G29.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Leo Bodnar BU0836A USB Joystick Interface (soldered)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The exact board the Bridge Command DIY guide names. Local file exists but UNVERIFIED and was removed. The verified solderless BU0836X stands in as the DIY-board visual; a real BU0836A photo would let the Bridge Command reference show the precise board.

removedUnverifiedNote: Prior attempt embedded 5 unverified gear images (a shotgrid of 4 + 2 gearpick shots, with the G29 and Saitek quadrant appearing twice each). All unverified references were stripped. The shotgrid was dropped entirely because 2 of its 4 members were unverified; the surviving verified members are shown as inline gearpicks instead. Only the verified 9 remain in the body.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Dark-slate workbench under teal rim light, an empty helm mount and open panel cutout waiting to be filled, a repurposed racing wheel, a flight throttle quadrant and a bare DIY interface board laid out beside it as if a builder is deciding what to press into service as boat controls, no fake marine product, no fake branded UI, honest maker-bench mood, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake boat-wheel product, fake logos, warped controls, unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: What people actually use (and what it was built for)
content: Bridge panel = button box / DIY board
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, real product photos as row markers for verified items only, text-only labels for the racing wheel and throttle quadrant until photos are verified

infographicPrompts.title: The analog-axis gate
content: Sim reads analog axes? -> wheel + throttle work
Keyboard-only? -> wheel = binary L/R, throttle dead
Check the store page BEFORE you buy
infographicPrompts.style: Decision-flow diagram, marine teal accents, large readable text

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder at a maker bench, wrapping a loop of string around a potentiometer shaft to add throttle friction, practical hands-on energy, DIY board and 10k pots on the bench, no fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
